title: 怪钟疑案
summary:
Detective Inspector Hakast examines a collection of items found near the deceased man, including a wallet containing seven pounds and ten shillings, a silk handkerchief, digestive pills, and a business card for R. H. Kouri, Esq., of the Great City Small Town Insurance Company at 7 Danver Street, London. Hakast questions Miss Pema-Chhoe regarding these items, but she denies having any appointments with insurance companies, specifically stating she has never heard of the Metropolitan District Insurance Company and has no need for life insurance. She also denies knowing anyone named Kirby.

To assist the investigation, the visually impaired Miss Pema-Chhoe agrees to "feel" the dead man's face to attempt an identification. After tracing his hair, eyes, ears, nose, mouth, and chin, she concludes she does not recognize him. Once the body of the man, identified by the card as Mr. Kouri, is moved from the room, Hakast reviews the facts with her. Miss Pema-Chhoe reiterates that she had no insurance consultations and made no requests for stenographer services from the Calverdish Document Service. She notes that when she cleaned the house that morning, only a cuckoo clock and a grandfather clock were present. She clarifies her schedule, stating she was at Allenberg College from 10:00 to 12:15 and returned home at 12:45 before leaving again at 1:30.

Regarding the four new clocks found in the room with their hands set to 4:13, Miss Pema-Chhoe claims the time means nothing to her. After dismissing Miss Webber and Mr. Collins, Hakast instructs that the four clocks be packed for transport. Hakast then departs with Colin Lamb, heading toward the Cavendish Manuscript Society on the Strand.
